title: Crispy Sour Japanese Miso Gyoza
description: A crowdpleasing, sour and crispy gyoza featuring a unique white masarepa dough and a misovinegar filling, made paleofriendly for a healthy twist.

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: add more if dough is too dry

instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine white masarepa flour, sesame oil, and egg white. Gradually add water until a firm but pliable dough forms. Let rest for 10 minutes.
In another bowl, mix ground pork, shredded cabbage, grated carrot, white miso paste, rice vinegar, and grated ginger. Season with a pinch of salt if desired.
Roll the dough into a thin sheet (about 3 mm thick). Cut into 3 cm circles using a round cookie cutter.
Place a teaspoon of filling onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over to form half‑moons, pinch edges to seal tightly, and pleat the edges for a traditional look.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat. Add 1 tbsp vegetable oil.
Place gyoza in the skillet, flat side down. Cook until bottoms are golden brown, about 2 minutes.
Add 2 tbsp water to the skillet, cover immediately to steam. Cook for another 3 minutes until steam has evaporated and filling is cooked through.
Remove the lid, let gyoza crisp for an additional minute, then transfer to a plate.
Serve hot with a dipping sauce made from soy sauce, rice vinegar, and a splash of sesame oil. Garnish with chopped scallions and sesame seeds if desired.
